response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with a strong track record in web platforms, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his profile has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload analysis — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest adjacent experience is low-latency software optimization at Dunnhumby and some C/C++ work at National Instruments, but neither approaches the hardware architecture and AI accelerator domain this role requires.
strengths: ~12 years of professional software engineering experience, satisfying the education/experience threshold for a Bachelor's degree path
Demonstrated performance-aware engineering: async I/O, caching, and high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second
Breadth across embedded systems, mobile, and low-level C/C++ (National Instruments, robotic arms) shows some comfort below the application layer
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or any AI accelerator hardware — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning training or inference optimization; AI exposure is limited to recent MCP/agentic tooling work, not hardware-level DNN performance
Current and historical work is almost entirely application-layer (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), with no hardware-software co-design or system architecture at the chip or silicon level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No AI/DL workload or accelerator expertise
